sections.paragraph:
f. (rarely masc. Isa 17:5 51:5 Dan 11:15, 22 Dan 11:22, especially in the signification No. 2. Comp. Lehrg. p. 470), m. pl. ־ִים and ־ֹות.

(1) an arm, Isa 17:5 40:11 especially the fore arm, as in Lat. brachium κατʼ ἐξοχὴν (differing from קָנֶה lacertus), Job 26:2 in animals the fore leg, shoulder, βραχίων, Num 6:19 Deu 18:3. (Arabic ذِرَاعُ, Aram. דְּרָעָא, ܕܪܳܥܐܳ an arm, also a cubit, from the root זָרַע No. 1). זְרֹועַ נְטוּיָה a stretched out arm, a gesture of threatening applied to a people ready for battle [“ascribed to God”], Exo 6:6 Deu 4:34 Eze 20:33, 34 Eze 20:34similarly זְרֹעַ רָמָה Job 38:15.

(2) Figuratively-(a) strength, might, power, Ch. 32:8 , זְרֹועַ בָּשָׂר “human power.” Psa 44:4 Job 40:9, זְרֹועֵי יָדָיו “the strength of his hands;” Gen 49:24. Hence military force, an army, Dan 11:15, 22 Dan 11:22, 31 Dan 11:31.-(b) violence, Job 35:9. אִישׁ זְרֹועַ “a violent man;” Job 22:8. Here the phrase belongs to break the arm of any one, for to destroy his power, or violence, 1Sa 2:31 Job 22:9 38:15 Psa 10:15 37:17 (comp. Arab. فتّ عظده).-(c) strength imparted to another in aiding him, hence help, aid. Psa 83:9 Isa 33:2 (like the Arab. عظد Pers. بازو an arm, also aid; Syr. ܒܰܪ ܕܪܳܥܐܳ son of arm, i.e. helper; see farther on the place referred to in Isaiah), hence a helper, a companion, Isa 9:19 (comp. Jer 9:19, where for this word is found רֵעַ). LXX. Cod. Alex. ἀδελφός. אֶזְרֹועַ is the same word with Aleph prosthetic.
